concepts.title: Linear Momentum
concepts.body: Learners should be able to define linear momentum and apply its principles, including the conservation of momentum in various scenarios.

children.title: Definition of Linear Momentum
children.body: Define linear momentum as the product of mass and velocity.

children.title: Force, Mass, and Acceleration (F=ma)
children.body: Solve problems using the relationship F=ma, appreciating that acceleration and force are always in the same direction.

children.title: Principle of Conservation of Momentum
children.body: State the principle of conservation of momentum.

children.title: Applications of Conservation of Momentum in Collisions
children.body: Use the principle of conservation of momentum on simple applications including elastic and inelastic collisions between two bodies in one dimension (calculations involving the use of coefficient of restitution are not required).

children.title: Perfectly Elastic Collisions
children.body: Recognise that, for a perfectly elastic collision, the relative speed of approach is equal to the relative speed of separation.

concepts.title: Impulse
concepts.body: Learners should be able to define impulse and understand its relationship with momentum and force-time graphs.

children.title: Significance of Area Under a Force-Time Graph
children.body: Explain the significance of area under a force - time graph.

concepts.title: Applications of Newton's Laws and Conservation of Linear Momentum
concepts.body: Learners should be able to describe various applications of Newton's laws of motion and the principle of conservation of linear momentum.

children.title: Applications of Newton's Laws of Motion
children.body: Describe applications of Newton's laws of motion.

children.title: Applications of Conservation of Linear Momentum
children.body: Describe applications of conservation of linear momentum.
